title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Concerned that their garage may have been compromised, Frankie and Bobby decide to meet at the DeWinter city residence on Brook Street. Frankie shares new details regarding the day Bobby was poisoned: while Barclay-French was in Stavely, Dr. Nicholson was not in London for a scheduled conference as claimed, and he was driving a deep blue Talbot. Bobby notes that Nicholson's ability to obtain morphine aligns with their ongoing suspicions.

Searching a phone book for the name "Rivington," Frankie identifies Colonel Rivington, a recipient of the Distinguished Service Cross living on Tite Street in Chelsea, as a potential lead. To investigate, Frankie instructs Bobby to impersonate a new partner at a law firm. She provides him with a business card for "Spragg-Spragg-Jenkinson & Spragg, Esqs." of Bloomsbury Square, which belongs to her father's lawyers. To complete the disguise, Bobby borrows a morning suit and striped trousers from the Marquess of Cheldon’s wardrobe.

Bobby travels to Tite Street and uses the fake business card to gain entry to the Rivington home. He meets Mrs. Rivington, who is swayed by his professional appearance and a fabricated claim that his office called ahead. Bobby pretends to represent their client, Mr. Allen Castles. Mrs. Rivington confirms that Castles is staying at the Savoy Hotel and mentions that the last time they saw him was at Stafholt, after which they visited the Bassington-Frenches. She also notes that Castles likely came to England because of a millionaire friend who died by suicide following a cancer diagnosis.
